LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MITCHELL TICH'S MOTION TO VACATE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT. MITCHELL TICH'S "MOTION TO VACATE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT" IS GRANTED. AS PLAINTIFF RECOGNIZES, TICH'S CCP 683.170 "MOTION IS MADE ON THE GROUNDS THAT HE WAS NEVER SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT" - GROUNDS SUPPORTED BY DECLARATIONS FROM TICH AND HIS FATHER. (OPP. 3:17-23.) PLAINTIFF'S PAPERS DO NOT SHOW SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ON TICH - E.G., BY EVIDENCE FROM A PROCESS-SERVER. WITHOUT THAT, THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION OVER TICH. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment (
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MITCHELL TICH'S Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment.

Mitchell Tich's "motion to vacate renewal of judgment" is granted. As plaintiff recognizes, Tich's CCP 683.170 "motion is made on the grounds that he was never served with the summons and complaint" - grounds supported by declarations from Tich and his father. (Opp. 3:17-23.) Plaintiff's papers do not show service of the summons and complaint on Tich - e.g., by evidence from a process-server. Without that, the court lacks jurisdiction over Tich.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
